pi_items.source: Task description line 1 and task.json metadata match exactly. Synthetic test data created by task designer.

Verified via Magento 2 and HTTP specifications. 301 is the standard HTTP status code for 'Moved Permanently' redirects. Magento url_rewrite table redirect_type column accepts 301 for permanent redirects (https://www.mgt-commerce.com/blog/magento-2-url-rewrite-table/).

Counted expected_rewrites array in task.json metadata. Verifier code (verifier.py) iterates through all 5 rewrites in the expected_list and verifies each one.
Verified via setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which both query url_rewrite WHERE entity_type='custom' to record and verify custom rewrites. Task description specifies these are 'Custom rewrites'.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates 5 synthetic custom URL rewrites in Magento 2. All metadata values are verified and consistent with the task description. The 5 rewrites with their exact specifications are: (1) 'summer-sale-electronics' → 'electronics.html' (301 permanent), (2) 'old-clothing-catalog' → 'clothing.html' (301 permanent), (3) 'featured-yoga-gear' → 'yoga-mat-premium.html' (301 permanent), (4) 'flash-deals' → 'sports.html' (302 temporary), (5) 'legacy/home-decor' → 'home-garden.html' (301 permanent). HTTP status codes 301 (Moved Permanently) and 302 (Moved Temporarily) are correct per HTTP standards and Magento 2 database schema. The verifier checks for existence, exact target path match (case-insensitive), correct redirect type, and confirms at least 5 new custom rewrites were created (anti-gaming verification via database count delta).
